title: attribution — subprocessors
description: The Attribution module itself engages ZERO third-party subprocessors — every state transition happens within the Academorix platform. Attribution DATA flows downstream to the Marketing module (Wave 5), which engages ad-network processors (Google, Meta, TikTok, Microsoft, Snapchat) for conversion forwarding — those live in marketing::subprocessors.json. Similarly the Analytics module (Wave 5) engages processors like Segment / PostHog — those live in analytics::subprocessors.json. This file lists ZERO active subprocessors to be explicit about the module's boundary — attribution capture + storage is fully first-party.

downstream_subprocessors_referenced.used_for: Conversion forwarding to ad networks. Every conversion event snapshots the attribution profile and forwards to Google Ads CAPI + Meta CAPI + TikTok Events API + Microsoft Ads Conversions API + Snapchat CAPI. The attribution profile IS the payload — but the marketing module owns the wire boundary.
Behavioral event capture. Every analytics track/identify/page event snapshots the attribution profile as a property. Processors: Segment, PostHog, Amplitude (per-tenant configurable).
Referral click matching. When the referrals module detects a referral-link click, it appends an attribution touchpoint via this module. No external processor engaged.

Dispatching AttributionResetConfirmationNotification + AttributionMergeCompletedNotification + AttributionSuppressedByConsentNotification + AttributionChurnDetectedNotification via mail / SMS / push channels.

notes: This file exists deliberately even with an empty subprocessors array. The GDPR Art. 30 record of processing activities requires per-module subprocessor lists; an empty list is the correct declaration for a module that engages none directly.

The attribution module is the source of truth for the frozen attribution snapshot. Downstream modules engage the ad networks and analytics processors — those subprocessor engagements are declared in the downstream modules, not here. This keeps the DPA (Data Processing Agreement) with each tenant precise about which module handles which subprocessor.

If a future Wave introduces a subprocessor into this module (e.g. a data-enrichment provider for reverse-IP-to-company lookup), add it here + update compliance.json Art. 28 evidence + refresh the tenant-facing subprocessor list. Currently there are NO plans for such enrichment — it would violate the module's data-minimisation stance.
